reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND on-page in headless Chrome; first-visit Download → real audio, reload auto-inits with no button; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED twice (headless Chrome, WASM). Standalone probe: pipeline('text-to-speech','Xenova/mms-tts-deu',{dtype:'q8'}) on 'Diese Stimme wird vollständig in Ihrem Browser erzeugt.' returned a real Float32Array — 77056 samples @ 16000 Hz (4.82 s), peak amplitude 0.743, mean |amp| 0.068 (non-silent). On the built page: fresh profile showed the Download state + button, clicking it reached state='ready', and pressing Sprechen produced real audio — 89,344 samples, 5.58 s, gen 6.53 s, real-time factor 1.17×, player src set — with ZERO console errors and ZERO failed network requests.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = { audio: Float32Array (mono), sampling_rate: 16000 }. The probe and the page both returned exactly that shape; the page encodes the Float32 PCM to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio> and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent German speech (peak 0.74).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language VITS demo: the German checkpoint (own German char vocab + German-trained weights), materially different from the built English mms-tts (Xenova/mms-tts-eng).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-deu, linked to its card), the original (facebook/mms-tts-deu), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB) and license (CC-BY-NC 4.0). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) noted in at-a-glance AND the footer byline.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture. Verified on-page: first visit (fresh profile) showed state='download-required' with a Download button; after downloading + validating, a reload took the auto-init path — state reached 'ready' with NO Download button rendered (reloadAutoInitNoDownload=true).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-deu so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Console + network stayed clean across first-visit download and cached reload.
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the German textarea, German sample chips (Vorstellung/Zungenbrecher/Umlaute/…), the Sprechen button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains a translator + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built English mms-tts demo.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The standalone probe confirmed the exact non-silent audio the UI plays.

All buttons have accessible names; the canvas carries aria-label='Generated German speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (dark screenshot captured).

Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) and clean desktop (1280×800) in headless Chrome. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Screenshots captured at both classes; support seeded via responsive-check.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop. Control UI stayed responsive during synthesis.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis is in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); console clean; UI responsive during 6.5 s synth

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360×740 in headless Chrome

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress and canvas labelling
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give the waveform canvas an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvas has aria-label; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: How much does the stochastic duration predictor vary German prosody vs English on the wild page — worth quantifying the spread distribution across German sentence types (punchy vs flowing).
Add more MMS long-tail languages as sibling distinct-language demos (e.g. a low-resource language with no commercial TTS) to make the 1000+ pitch concrete.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a low-resource MMS language demo (a language with no commercial TTS) to make the '1000+ languages, one recipe' claim tangible beyond German.
On the German wild page, add a duration-spread histogram across sentence types to visualise where the stochastic duration predictor samples most freely.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-german (Xenova/mms-tts-deu, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the GERMAN VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language demo vs the built English mms-tts: own German char vocab + German-trained weights, speaks ä/ö/ü/ß). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED standalone (77056 samples @16 kHz, peak 0.74, non-silent) AND on-page (first-visit Download → real 5.58 s audio, rtf 1.17×; reload auto-inits to ready with NO Download button; 0px mobile overflow; ZERO console/network errors). Ladder: basics (type German, hear it), practical (German narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (MarianMT en→de translate, then MMS speaks the German). Note: MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
